channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine won’t enter peace talks with Russia until it has security guarantees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Ukraine won’t enter peace talks with Russia until it has security guarantees against another offensive. Zelenskyy added Friday's contentious spat with President Donald Trump was “not good for both sides.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jury finds Illinois landlord guilty of murder, hate crime in 2023 attack on Palestinian American boy
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — A jury found an Illinois landlord guilty of murder and hate crime charges Friday for the brutal killing of a 6-year-old whose mother rented rooms in the man’s home, an attack that spiked fears over anti-Muslim discrimination in the earliest days of the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and MELISSA PEREZ WINDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Iowa's governor signs a bill removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa became the first U.S. state to remove gender identity protections from its civil rights code on Friday when Gov. Kim Reynolds signed into law a bill that opponents say will expose transgender people and other Iowans to discrimination in all aspects of daily life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Trump's order making English the official language in the US could mean
item.description: As President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States, activists and advocacy groups are alarmed by what that will mean for non-English speakers when it comes to immigration, voter access and other issues.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alabama governor commutes death row inmate Rocky Myers' sentence to life in prison
item.description: Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey has commuted the death sentence of Robin “Rocky” Myers to life in prison after noting questions about his case.
item.dc:creator: Kim Chandler, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Peak bloom for the iconic cherry blossom trees of Washington, DC, is predicted for late March
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For decades, the thousands of blooming cherry blossom trees in the nation’s capital have served as both a magnet to visitors and the unofficial start of the tourist season for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal weather and oceans agency touches people's daily lives in unnoticed ways, so massive firings there will likely cause needless deaths and a big hit to America's economy, according to the people who ran it.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: School bus caught on camera engulfed in flames after driver's ‘quick response’ saves students from inferno
item.description: An Ohio school bus driver was credited with saving 15 middle school students as a bus went up in flames. The inferno was caught on camera.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans target SNAP in federal spending cuts; food banks brace for impact
item.description: As House Republicans push to cut billions from the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), commonly known as food stamps, food banks are bracing for potential impacts.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, are back in the US: What to know
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, arrived Thursday in the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions on the siblings, who have millions of online followers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope had bronchial spasm, resulted in him inhaling vomit, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Law school project finds slavery citations still being used today
item.description: EAST LANSING, Mich. (AP) — An 1842 U.S. Supreme Court ruling overturning the kidnapping conviction of a white man who seized a Black family and forced them into slavery south of the Mason-Dixon line is still being cited in American jurisprudence, 160 years after enslaved people throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Vance go off on Zelensky in contentious Oval Office spat
item.description: President Trump went off on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during an extraordinary Oval Office meeting in front of cameras on Friday in which he raised his voice and called the foreign leader “disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M boat with crew on board
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Horse falls through ice, gets stuck in New York pond
item.description: A horse was in imminent danger of freezing to death after it fell through ice and got stuck in a pond in New York on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man charged after allegedly faking seizure as puppies were stolen
item.description: A 37-year-old man has been formally charged for his alleged role in the theft of two bulldogs from a Centennial pet store.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, NewsNation

item.title: Once-dominant video conferencing service shutting down
item.description: Skype, a groundbreaking service in the world of internet-based communications, will shut down after more than two decades of operations, parent company Microsoft announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Texas measles cases rise to 146 in an outbreak that led to a child's death
item.description: Texas health officials say the number of people with measles has increased to 146 in rural West Texas outbreak that led this week to the death of a school-aged child who was not vaccinated.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal agencies plan second email asking workers for weekly accomplishments
item.description: Some agencies plan to send a second Saturday email to federal workers asking them for bullet points on what they did this week, a sign at least some departments plan to continue with the controversial practice ignited by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: A stretch of a North Carolina highway that collapsed during Helene is about to reopen
item.description: A stretch of Interstate 40 through the western North Carolina mountains is reopening to traffic this weekend, months after Hurricane Helene's historic flooding collapsed portions of the road, partially restoring the major travel connection with eastern Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom, pave part of the Rose Garden
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to add a ballroom to the White House and to pave part the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M fishing boat with crew still on board, officials say
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: Vice President JD Vance, a Catholic, acknowledges the pope's criticism of US immigration crackdown
item.description: Vice President JD Vance acknowledged Pope Francis’ criticism of the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigration, without responding to any of its specifics or to the pontiff’s apparent criticism of Vance’s own deployment of Catholic tradition to justify such policies.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Midwest winters are changing. So is the ancient sport of falconry
item.description: GREENLEAF, Wis. (AP) — Stephanie Stevens has a good reason to love the bone-numbing cold of a Wisconsin winter. Every weekend, she loads up her minivan with a large green box and drives out to rural areas, usually the edges of friends' farm fields.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anti-Israel protesters at Barnard College arrested after mob turns violent
item.description: Protests continued another day after a group of more than 50 anti-Israel protesters took over a building at New York City's Barnard College on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College students create dating app with safety as top priority
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) -- As if college students didn’t already have enough on their plates with classes and studying, a group of students at Ohio State came together to create a new app called DuoDate.
item.dc:creator: Gracie Collins, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NOAA begins firing hundreds of staffers
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) began the process of firing hundreds of probationary workers Thursday, the latest mass job cuts within federal agencies, three sources with knowledge of internal operations told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WGN 9

item.title: How to save your video: Facebook to automatically delete live videos after 30 days
item.description: Facebook is changing its live video feature, allowing users to keep their videos for 30 days after publishing, after which they will be automatically deleted from Meta servers.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Thompson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Texas says this doctor illegally treated trans youth. He says he followed the law
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (AP) — On the Texas border, Dr. Hector Granados treats children with diabetes at his El Paso clinics and makes hospital rounds under the shadow of accusations that have thrown his career into jeopardy: providing care to transgender youth. In what's believed to be a U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa were dead for some time, found in different rooms, investigators say
item.description: A spokesperson for the state’s medical examiner declined to comment on whether any conclusions had been reached on the cause.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, WGN 9

item.title: McDonald's will not add egg surcharge as $1 Egg McMuffin Day announced
item.description: McDonalds is not adding a surcharge to their meals with eggs as they celebrate 50 years of the Egg McMuffin.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court hears arguments on straight woman's 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Child becomes first measles death reported in Texas outbreak
item.description: An unvaccinated school-aged child who was hospitalized with measles has passed away, marking the first death in the outbreak, according to Lubbock and state health officials.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, ABC 4

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
